risk_flags: llm_fallback

chapter_function: Farewell chapter demonstrating protagonist virtue through citizens' testimony and failed pleas, while foreshadowing court danger and initiating the capital journey arc

state_changes.before: County magistrate in final administrative duties
state_changes.after: Promoted official (内升吏部都给事) traveling to capital with official escort
state_changes.story_value: establishes moral authority and political elevation

state_changes.before: Citizens mourning and resisting departure
state_changes.after: Citizens reluctantly accepting and giving tearful farewell with material tribute
state_changes.story_value: emotional payoff for demonstrated virtue

state_changes.before: Threat mentioned but vague
state_changes.after: Specific danger from 卢贼 introduced as political obstacle
state_changes.story_value: raises stakes for capital arrival

reader_rewards: Witnessing cathartic farewell ritual with collective emotional release
Direct testimony of protagonist's governance achievements through multiple citizen voices
Foreshadowing of court political dangers through villain warning
Irony of elaborate official reception at humble roadside inn
Protagonist's moral philosophy expressed through parting advice and couplet
setups: Villain (卢贼) will pose threat at court
Elaborate inn setup suggests ominous planning
Protagonist's upcoming conversation with official runners
payoffs: Citizens' accumulated testimony validates protagonist's virtue
Villain threat explicitly raised by citizens
Elaborate inn prepared exactly as needed
open_hooks: What is 卢贼's identity and political power?
Why is the inn prepared so precisely?
What will the runners reveal about capital politics?
What awaits protagonist at court?
reusable_mechanisms: Crowning ceremony farewell pattern: citizens offer tribute (万民衣伞), protagonist gives parting advice, tearful parting
Collective voice testimony: multiple citizens speak in unified praise while each adding specific detail
Villain-warning-through-loyal-subjects: sympathetic characters raise danger that protagonist must risk anyway
Dramatic irony reception: elaborate official preparations at humble setting signal hidden complexity
Protagonist monologue on duty: refusal to hide, acceptance of consequence, prioritization of loyalty
Protagonist virtue shown through opposite characters: corrupt officials vs. grateful citizens

craft_observations.pov_distance: Third-person limited with occasional omniscient summary; follows protagonist closely but gives full citizen dialogue
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Classical Chinese balanced parallel sentences in dialogue; shorter punchy narrative sentences for scene changes
craft_observations.paragraphing: Long dialogue exchanges unbroken by narration; scene breaks marked by transition phrases
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Extensive direct speech with honorific markers; characters identified by role before speaking
craft_observations.description_method: Sparse environmental description; character details emerge through action and speech rather than narration
craft_observations.exposition_method: Backstory delivered through character speeches rather than narrator summary
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Chapter ends mid-conversation with anticipation of revelation; previous chapter ended with dramatic farewell request
craft_observations.transition_method: Time skip markers (次日早晨, 不多时); spatial transitions through travel action
do_not_copy: Direct translation of classical couplet structure
Specific honorific vocabulary patterns
Identical farewell ceremony choreography

non_reusable_specifics: Magistrate Mei (梅公) name and title
Lu thief (卢贼) as antagonist figure
Wanmin clothing and umbrella (万民衣伞) tribute artifacts
Longevity memorial tablets (长生牌位)
Jinan prefecture and Licheng county (济南府历城县) locations
Changzhou (常州) as destination
Libu Dudushi (吏部都给事) official rank
Mei Bai (梅白) servant name
Specific couplet: 业可养身须着惜, 事非干己莫劳心
All poem contents and classical verse
Ten-li pavilion (十里长亭) farewell location
City god temple (城隍庙) farewell venue
risk_flags: Heavy reliance on dialogue over action may test pacing tolerance
Citizens' extended praise through collective voice risks didactic tone
Contrast between tearful farewell and ominous inn may feel tonally disjoint
Protagonist's moral certainty expressed through others' confession may seem unearned to modern readers
No physical conflict or tension in chapter despite political foreshadowing

chapter_function: Transitional chapter that relocates the disguised protagonist to a new household while revealing key backstory through dialogue, establishing romantic tension through a brief visual encounter.

state_changes.before: Estranged, alone on road facing death
state_changes.after: Hidden in temple under monk's protection
state_changes.story_value: Survival secured but identity remains concealed

state_changes.before: Desperate fugitive with no prospects
state_changes.after: Employed servant in minister's household
state_changes.story_value: New social connection established through deception

state_changes.before: Protagonist is anonymous young man in disguise
state_changes.after: Reader learns monk is former military marshal who abandoned court due to corruption
state_changes.story_value: Contextual worldbuilding achieved

state_changes.before: No female character introduced
state_changes.after: Protagonist observes minister's daughter
state_changes.story_value: Triangle or romantic subplot initiated

reader_rewards: Monk's full backstory: former military hero turned Buddhist after witnessing court corruption
Explanation of political villainy causing the protagonist's father's downfall
First visual introduction of the love interest
Demonstration of protagonist's calligraphy skill establishing hidden cultivation
setups: Protagonist's suicide attempt
Monk's solitary temple existence
Minister's ruined estate needing restoration
payoffs: Rescue provides new庇护
Monk's brother arrives to offer employment
Protagonist sees the minister's daughter
open_hooks: Will the protagonist maintain his disguise in the minister's household?
Does the minister know the protagonist's true identity?
What role will the love interest play in the protagonist's revenge plans?
Will the monk truly return to the mountains?
reusable_mechanisms: Rescue sequence using coincidental discovery by peripheral character
Identity concealment maintained through humble servant role
Backstory delivered through conversation between two characters who already know it
Incidental character introduction leading to household employment
Visual attraction established through overhearing others discuss the new arrival
Physical description withheld until dramatic reveal moment

craft_observations.pov_distance: Close third-person following protagonist's perspective with omniscient intrusions for backstory exposition
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Classical Chinese prose style with balanced parallel constructions in poetic verses; narrative prose uses short declarative sentences for action, longer explanatory sentences for context
craft_observations.paragraphing: Scene breaks indicated by formulaic transitions; dialogue paragraphs alternate between speakers without tags after initial identification
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Direct speech with minimal attribution tags; speakers identified through context and relationship descriptors
craft_observations.description_method: Essential physical details given only at key moments (hanging body, love interest's appearance); most description focuses on actions and dialog
craft_observations.exposition_method: Backstory emerges naturally through character conversation rather than narrative summary; present events move quickly while past events receive detailed explanation
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Chapter ends mid-sentence with anticipation of next scene, creating natural page-turn
craft_observations.transition_method: Time skip marked by temporal formula (two months); spatial transitions handled by character movement (walking, being escorted)

non_reusable_specifics: Character names: 梅良玉/王喜童, 香池和尚, 陈日高, 陈东初, 杏元
Location names: 扬州, 寿庵寺, 山阳关, 常州府
Organizational details: Temple hierarchy with长老 and化跎和尚; ministerial household with公子 and 小姐
Historical setting: Specific to Chinese imperial court with 胡虏鞑靼 conflicts, 三边总制 military position
Artifact specifics: 箱笼 luggage, 盆景花草 ornamental plants, 姜汤 ginger soup
risk_flags: Backstory exposition could stall narrative momentum
Monk's extended backstory delivered in single block may test reader patience
Romantic subplot introduction feels rushed after survival sequence
Protagonist's passive situation (being given away by one master to another) may reduce agency perception

chapter_function: Tragic loyalty payoff with despair escalation - loyal servant's sacrificial death is witnessed by master, who then journeys forward and reaches rock-bottom despair at chapter's end.

state_changes.before: alive, attempting to protect master through disguise
state_changes.after: dead by arsenic poisoning in prison
state_changes.story_value: noble sacrifice fulfilled, master bears witness to loyal friend's death

state_changes.before: had trusted companion to share burden
state_changes.after: alone, orphaned in travels, stripped of companion
state_changes.story_value: deepened isolation intensifies desperation

state_changes.before: had clothing and ability to pay
state_changes.after: exchanged clothing for passage, arrives with nothing
state_changes.story_value: complete material destitution mirrors spiritual collapse

state_changes.before: uncertain direction, fleeing county
state_changes.after: arrived Yangzhou but aimless and friendless
state_changes.story_value: geographic goal reached without resolution of core conflict

state_changes.before: seeking reunion with mother, harboring hope for justice
state_changes.after: contemplating self-termination at temple threshold
state_changes.story_value: psychological breaking point reached

reader_rewards: Payoff of loyal friend's ultimate sacrifice witnessed firsthand
Emotional catharsis through extended mourning at graveside
Gratifying scene of humble benefactors treating protagonist with dignity
Satisfying contrast between past prosperity and present abjection
Dramatic irony in master now understanding friend's secret wisdom too late
setups: Servant's purchase of poison at earlier point
Master's naive trust in father's potential allies
False prince's capture and subsequent imprisonment
Mother-son separation as unresolved牵挂 thread
Two elders' incidental appearance on boat
payoffs: Servant's loyal-death fulfillment completed
Master finally recognizes friend's wisdom after it's too late
Protagonist's journey to Yangzhou achieved
Material poverty now complete
open_hooks: Will protagonist actually complete suicide attempt
Who are the two elders who showed unexpected kindness
Will protagonist be recognized as wanted fugitive in Yangzhou
What becomes of the false prince under interrogation
Will mother-son reunion ever occur
reusable_mechanisms: Loyal-follower self-sacrifice for master - emotional peak payoff
Stranger benefactors appearing at moment of maximum need - emotional relief beat
Rock-bottom despair with suicide contemplation as chapter-ending cliffhanger
Journey between locations with embedded micro-dramas
Identity-double caught leading to cascading consequences
Graveside emotional outpouring as grief processing ritual
Complete loss of possessions and dignity before story turning point

craft_observations.pov_distance: Close identification with protagonist's emotional state, reader internalizes fear and grief through direct access to thoughts and tears
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Alternates between extended emotional paragraphs of grief and rapid-fire dialogue exchanges; grief passages slow dramatically while action scenes accelerate
craft_observations.paragraphing: Long unbroken emotional paragraphs for mourning and despair contrasted with short punchy lines for dialogue and sudden events like poisoning
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Functional dialogue serves plot advancement and characterization; minimal witty exchange, mostly necessary information transfer
craft_observations.description_method: Sparse physical description; emotional interior dominates; setting serves psychological state not independent atmosphere
craft_observations.exposition_method: Character thoughts reveal backstory organically through grief rather than info-dump; no external narrator exposition needed
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Final scene dangles suicide attempt with orison to absent mother; immediate crisis resolved with question mark
craft_observations.transition_method: Location shifts marked by clear physical movement: cemetery to road to river to city to temple; protagonist follows clear geographic progression

non_reusable_specifics: Xi Tong and Mei Liangyu character names
False prince and magistrate Huan Luan
Yangzhou as destination city
Arsenic as poison method
Shou An Temple
The two unidentified elder benefactors
North gate and ferry as route
Guangling region geography
risk_flags: Suicide scene as chapter-ending hook - requires careful handling
Protagonist at absolute emotional and material low point may alienate readers seeking agency
Lengthy grief processing may test pacing tolerance
Committed suicide of sympathetic character could register as punitive despite narrative justification
llm_fallback

chapter_function: Establishment chapter: protagonist settles into new identity while discovering the household's connection to his family's tragedy, creating dramatic irony through incomplete information asymmetry
scene_functions: Protagonist receives social identity through adoption ritual
Protagonist demonstrates hidden talent (calligraphy) to gain household trust
Protagonist discovers his location's connection to father's death
Time passage through seasons leading to new gathering

state_changes.before: Stranger/rescued individual without established place
state_changes.after: Adopted son of household servant with recognized status
state_changes.story_value: Enables protagonist to remain in household while concealing true identity

state_changes.before: Unaware of household's connection to father's fate
state_changes.after: Partial awareness that this official was connected to family tragedy, but uncertain of his current disposition
state_changes.story_value: Creates dramatic tension through information asymmetry between protagonist and reader

state_changes.before: Protagonist has no designated role
state_changes.after: Assigned to garden maintenance, demonstrating skill in calligraphy for official correspondence
state_changes.story_value: Establishes protagonist's utility while maintaining subordinate status

state_changes.before: Early winter (immediately after rescue)
state_changes.after: New Year celebration passed, spring approaching, plum blossoms blooming
state_changes.story_value: Marks passage of time and signals transition to new narrative phase

reader_rewards: Revelation of protagonist's true identity dilemma: he is in the household of the official connected to his father's execution
Calligraphy skill demonstration shows hidden competence that will become significant
Adoption subplot establishes surrogate family relationships that will complicate loyalty conflicts
Dramatic irony: protagonist decides to conceal his identity 'for now' while we know more than he does about his situation
setups: Official family structure: two brothers, one a military official turned monk, one a former minister
Protagonist's concealment of his real name and background
Protagonist's emotional reaction to discovering the connection (tears quickly suppressed)
Servant couple's investment in protagonist as their future heir
open_hooks: Will the protagonist reveal his true identity? To whom? When?
What was the father's 'involvement' with Official B that led to the father's execution?
Will the protagonist encounter the young miss again at the plum blossom gathering?
How will the servant couple's investment in finding him a wife affect the protagonist's situation?
What is Official B's current 'heart attitude' toward the events that destroyed the protagonist's family?
reusable_mechanisms: Information revelation through casual conversation: protagonist learns critical background by asking innocent questions
Skill demonstration without self-promotion: protagonist's value recognized by others based on observable output
Suppressed emotional reaction: protagonist controls visible response to shocking information, maintaining cover
Adoption as narrative positioning: protagonist gains household access and social legitimacy without revealing true status
Seasonal marker as structural transition: plum blossoms signal new social gathering and potential plot development
Time compression through summary: 'a month' compressed after arrival, showing settling-in period quickly
Double perspective: narration follows protagonist's limited knowledge while reader may infer more

craft_observations.pov_distance: Close third-person following protagonist's perceptions; reader gains access to internal thoughts through direct reflection ('he thought to himself')
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Mixed register: classical poetry opening establishes formal tone, followed by conversational dialogue and narration; short punchy sentences for dramatic moments, longer sentences for scene-setting
craft_observations.paragraphing: Scene breaks unmarked; natural flow between dialogue and action; significant revelations given space through pause beats (protagonist stopping to reflect)
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Heavy reliance on servant character conveying information to protagonist and reader simultaneously; some dialogue for characterization, most for exposition
craft_observations.description_method: Minimal physical description; focuses on social roles and emotional states; garden mentioned in passing as functional space
craft_observations.exposition_method: Information delivered through conversation between servant and protagonist; official's background given in extended speech block
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Chapter ends with command to prepare plum blossom gathering—imminent social event that will force protagonist into closer contact with official family
craft_observations.transition_method: Time passage signaled through seasonal references and explicit summary ('a month passed'); new chapter indicated by plum blossom planning

non_reusable_specifics: Protagonist's birth name (refers to father executed for 'official business')
Two officials' names, former titles, and family details
Servant couple's names and domestic arrangements
Poem text and ci (song lyric) form opening
Specific calligraphy types listed (年家眷弟, 姻眷弟, 眷侍生)
Plum blossom and lantern festival references
risk_flags: Heavy exposition through single character speech may slow pacing
Protagonist's internal conflict not yet fully dramatized through action
Multiple relationship threads (adopted son, potential marriage, garden work) may dilute focus
Information asymmetry could frustrate readers wanting faster revelation

state_changes.after: existential despair, conviction to abandon worldly life

state_changes.before: flourishing plum blossoms representing deceased friend

state_changes.before: servant conceals true lineage as executed official's son
state_changes.after: emotional breakdown partially reveals inner conflict
state_changes.story_value: plants suspicion in observant female protagonist

state_changes.before: harmonious gathering celebrating spring

state_changes.before: stable domestic scene with memorial plans
state_changes.after: three-day impossible challenge issued

reader_rewards: Partial reveal of servant's hidden noble identity
First meeting/observation moment between observant heroine and disguised youth
Ominous prophecy interpretation creating stakes for deceased friend's lineage
Test-based cliffhanger promising miracle or tragedy
Family dynamics displayed under crisis pressure
setups: Deceased official's execution anniversary
Servant protagonist's grief at hearing of father's death
Protagonist's history of official service and retirement
Observant heroine who notices emotional inconsistencies
Omen-reading interpretation framework
payoffs: Lady's suspicion of servant's noble bearing (unfulfilled)
Three-day challenge imposed on family (unfulfilled)
Protagonist's departure decision (unfulfilled)

reusable_mechanisms: Omen reading: natural destruction interpreted as cosmic judgment on person's fate
Secret identity concealment through servant position with emotional tell
Prayer challenge: supplication to supernatural forces with observable condition
Three-day deadline: arbitrary time constraint creating countdown tension
Family pleaders: relatives attempting to reverse departure decision
Symbolic object death-and-rebirth: destroyed symbol must magically restore

craft_observations.pov_distance: Third-person limited following Chen Gong's perspective, shifting to external observation of servant's tears
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Classical Chinese prose patterns with parallel couplets in verse sections, longer narrative sentences in prose
craft_observations.paragraphing: Scene-based paragraphs with clear breaks between location changes and time shifts
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Formal dialogue with honorifics and clear speaker tags; minimal subtext in surface conversation
craft_observations.description_method: Symbol-focused description where plum blossoms carry heavy symbolic weight beyond physical description
craft_observations.exposition_method: Dialogue-delivered backstory about execution date and memorial significance
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Final paragraph ends mid-prayer before any result, forcing page turn
craft_observations.transition_method: Scene break indicated by temporal marker '却说次日早晨' for next-day transition

non_reusable_specifics: Chen Gong as retired official with Chen Dongchu name
Mei Gong as executed friend from previous year
Xi Tong as secretly disguised son of executed official
Xingyuan as observant daughter with识人的眼睛
Three specific domains of wandering: mountain monasteries, river boats, village hermitages
February timing of plum blossom season and death anniversary
risk_flags: Cliffhanger depends entirely on miracle acceptance
Secret identity may be telegraphed too obviously through emotional response
Prayer mechanism lacks clear rules for success/failure conditions
Male protagonist departure motivation may feel melodramatic rather than earned
Female protagonist's intelligence not yet demonstrated through action only observation

chapter_function: Miracle payoff with simultaneous identity concealment - the prayed-for event occurs, but the true petitioner's identity remains hidden while his excessive grief nearly exposes him, and a new mystery (the wall poem) is introduced to sustain engagement.

state_changes.before: Plum blossoms destroyed by storm, 陈公 intends to abandon worldly life
state_changes.after: Plum blossoms miraculously bloom overnight, 陈公 abandons plan to leave home
state_changes.story_value: supernatural confirmation of sincere devotion; hope restored

state_changes.before: 喜童 maintains cover identity successfully through mundane interactions
state_changes.after: 喜童's extreme grief at the memorial nearly exposes him -跪拜 and weeping exceed servant behavior
state_changes.story_value: identity concealment under pressure; dramatic irony preserved but strained

state_changes.before: Readers know 喜童 is actually 梅良玉, the deceased梅公的 son
state_changes.after: 陈公 remains unaware but suspicious attention is drawn to喜童's anomalous behavior
state_changes.story_value: dramatic irony deepened; future exposure foreshadowed

state_changes.before: No wall poem exists
state_changes.after: Wall poem discovered - authorship unknown to characters, creates new mystery
state_changes.story_value: chapter ends with unresolved curiosity

reader_rewards: Supernatural prayer payoff demonstrating the power of filial devotion
Dramatic irony: readers witness both prayers while characters see only one
Near-miss identity exposure as tension builder
Hidden identity behind private grief at father's memorial
New mystery: the wall poem and its author
setups: 小姐's public prayer at the beginning of the chapter
喜童's secret prayer immediately following
陈公's declared intention to leave home if blossoms don't bloom
喜童's established backstory of losing his father
payoffs: 梅开二度 occurs through divine intervention
陈公 abandons his plan to become a Taoist
Both prayers are answered despite only one being visible to the household
喜童 nearly exposes himself through excessive grief but is excused
Wall poem discovered but not yet attributed
open_hooks: Who wrote the poem on the wall?
Will陈公 investigate?
How will喜童's identity remain concealed going forward?
What will happen to the poem when authorship is revealed?
reusable_mechanisms: Dual-layer prayer structure: two characters pray for same outcome for different reasons, creating dramatic irony when only one is visible to other characters
Supernatural intervention via celestial bureaucracy: prayer escalated through spirit hierarchy (花神→城隍→玉帝) with guaranteed response and delayed execution
Prayer-to-payoff compression: multi-day waiting period collapsed into single chapter for narrative momentum
Near-exposure through anomalous behavior: protagonist's authentic emotion exceeds cover-role expectations, drawing suspicion
Private grief ritual: protagonist conducts memorial for own family while praised for loyalty to another's family
Discovery device: miracle triggers physical investigation leading to unrelated discovery (the poem)
Excusal mechanism: observing character provides plausible alternative explanation for anomalous behavior, preserving cover temporarily

craft_observations.pov_distance: Third-person limited with strategic internal access; primarily follows喜童's inner turmoil while showing外部 reactions
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Alternates between extended narrative prose and direct dialogue; emotional scenes use shorter declarative sentences
craft_observations.paragraphing: Long descriptive paragraphs for supernatural scenes; staccato exchanges for dialogue-heavy tension moments
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Attributed dialogue with frequent emotion tags; much information conveyed through verbal confirmation of external events
craft_observations.description_method: Minimal physical description; heavy reliance on emotional states and verbal exchanges to convey plot progression
craft_observations.exposition_method: Character monologue for backstory (喜童's reflections on his journey); direct verbal exchange for confirming current events
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Chapter ends mid-scene with question addressed to喜童, creating imperative continuation
craft_observations.transition_method: Temporal jump compressed through dream-sequence香气袭人; spatial transitions follow character movement from亭子→内室→后堂→园中
do_not_copy: Specific prayer language
Exact prayer formulations
Particularized deity hierarchies

non_reusable_specifics: 杏元小姐's specific prayer wording and location
喜童's specific prayer wording and secret motivation
The wall poem's exact content
花神城隍玉帝 hierarchical structure specifics
陈公's specific dialogue about leaving home
喜童's specific backstory details about母舅任原
risk_flags: Prayer-to-miracle mechanism may feel unearned without sufficient buildup in prior chapters
Multiple prayers answered simultaneously could reduce sense of individual devotion's weight
Near-exposure followed by excusal may train readers to expect easy escapes
Wall poem discovery is somewhat convenient - investigation of miracle naturally leads to unrelated artifact
Cover identity relies on 陈公's forgiveness rather than protagonist's competence, which could undermine agency

chapter_function: Split-screen transitional chapter resolving one character arc while launching another; closes the fake-noble-lady deception thread and repositions the surviving heroine into a new family structure

state_changes.before: Falling toward death at border cliff
state_changes.after: Standing in Zou family garden, adopted as义女
state_changes.story_value: Survival confirmed; new domestic anchor established

state_changes.before: Fugitive impersonating a noble lady
state_changes.after: Recognized as virtuous maiden, accepted as daughter by Zou family
state_changes.story_value: Legitimate status reestablished under new family

state_changes.before: Being escorted as sacrifice to foreign king
state_changes.after: Presented to foreign king, accepted as real; thread closed for now
state_changes.story_value: Deception succeeds; consequences deferred

state_changes.before: Completing escort duty at border, preparing to return to capital
state_changes.after: Summoned by urgent messenger with unknown alarming news
state_changes.story_value: New crisis introduced; chapter ends on threat

reader_rewards: Survival confirmation for the heroine readers likely feared was dead
New sympathetic character (Yunying) introduced as sister figure
Resolution of the disguise deception (it works, temporarily)
Character chemistry established between Xingyuan and her new adopted family
Setup of ominous new threat via Dang Gong's alarming summons
setups: Zou family's existence and household composition (Zou Zaodi, wife Zheng, daughter Yunying)
Xingyuan's adopted-daughter status in Zou household
Foreign kingdom's belief in the fake noble lady
Dang Gong's alarming summons mid-journey
open_hooks: What crime or accusation awaits Dang Gong (implied by缇骑)
Will the fake noble lady's deception be exposed over time
Will Xingyuan's real father (汪日升) be found and reunited

reusable_mechanisms: Miraculous divine intervention saving a virtuous female character from suicide
Disguise-as-noble-lady deception that succeeds due to the deceiver's 'settling down'
Found family through adoption in crisis moment
Multiple plot threads physically separated and handled sequentially within one chapter
Chapter-ending alarm/cliffhanger using urgent messenger and pursuers
Classical poetry used as chapter epigraph establishing mood

craft_observations.pov_distance: Third-person omniscient narrator maintains comfortable distance, shifting between geographic locations and character groups without inhabiting any single consciousness
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Alternates between extended explanatory passages and short urgent declaratives; the final paragraph accelerates into clipped threat
craft_observations.paragraphing: Long stretches of unbroken dialogue with minimal stage direction; paragraph breaks signal major scene transitions
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Direct quotation with minimal attribution tags; relies on honorific language and formal speech patterns to distinguish speakers and social hierarchy
craft_observations.description_method: Sparingly applied; atmosphere conveyed through dialogue context rather than sensory detail
craft_observations.exposition_method: Character backstory delivered through formal speech when meeting new characters; the Zou family situation explained through the wife's questions
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Final paragraph introduces alarming news through external events (报马,缇骑) rather than character decision, creating external threat rather than internal conflict
craft_observations.transition_method: Poetic epigraph opens chapter; transitional phrases (按下不提, 不言) signal location shifts; cliffhanger interrupts ongoing journey
do_not_copy: The specific classical Chinese verse form as chapter opener
The particular phrase '富人多淫' as characterization shorthand
The exact naming convention (字/号 structure)

non_reusable_specifics: Zou Zaodi, Yunying, Zheng氏 character specifics
Cui Huan as specific character name
汪日升 as Xingyuan's father name
河北大名府邹家 as specific location
河南道御史 and 副都御史 as specific official titles
昭君娘娘 as specific divine entity responsible for rescue
秦金 as specific supporting character name
risk_flags: Four distinct plot threads in single chapter risks diffusion of focus
Divine rescue from suicide may discomfort some readers
Adoption solution to crisis could read as avoiding rather than resolving
Alarming summons hook is generic without specificity
Female characters defined primarily through domestic virtues and family roles

Rescue execution - officials retrieve the captured wife and arrest all perpetrators

Protagonist's unusual refinement for a fisherman
The magistrate's power and connections

craft_observations.pov_distance: Narration maintains third-person omniscient distance, freely entering the governor's thoughts and the family members' private conversation without interior monologue
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Classical Chinese prose rhythm with balanced four-character phrases, formal courtroom speech patterns contrasted with colloquial family dialogue
craft_observations.paragraphing: Long exposition blocks for backstory, direct discourse for dialogue, scene shifts marked by stage directions like "再言" and "单言"
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Extended formal courtroom exchanges with repeated questioning patterns; intimate family dialogue uses contractions and casual address
craft_observations.description_method: Minimal physical description; character assessment delivered through dialogue ("真正令人可爱"), moral character shown through speech patterns

craft_observations.hook_delivery: Chapter opens with classical verse, ends mid-sentence on the court summons, both beginning and end create forward momentum
craft_observations.transition_method: Scene transitions marked by narrative address ("再言", "单言"), temporal markers ("不多一会", "明日早起"), and spatial movement (from outer hall to inner chambers)
do_not_copy: Repeated formal address patterns ("青天大人")

craft_observations.pov_distance: 第三人称全知视角，叙事者通过夫人视角串联内外场景，形成观察-反应-行动的情感链条

craft_observations.paragraphing: 场景间用'话说''再言''于是'等标记过渡，保持古典章回体节奏感
craft_observations.dialogue_method: 对话承载全部关键信息，动作描写极少，依靠台词推进真相揭露
craft_observations.description_method: 极度节约，只用'形容消瘦''双目紧闭'等最简标记，主要依赖对话交代情境
craft_observations.exposition_method: 通过人物自述完成身世揭露，通过仆人议论补充背景

craft_observations.transition_method: 空间转移依靠'于是''才至''再言'等连接词，人物行动形成自然切割
do_not_copy: '闷似湘江水，涓涓不断流'类诗句开场

Specific: 河北大名府 and 山西太原府 as locations
Specific: 春生's parents imprisoned and sister的事件
Specific: 迎宾馆 as meeting place
risk_flags: Risk: antagonist's scheming shown through obvious setup (false summons), may strain believability
Risk: protagonist's internal monologue during confrontation could tip into exposition
Risk: marriage refusal scene risks repetitive verbal sparring without physical stakes
Risk: multiple named characters in quick succession may confuse reader tracking
Risk: villain's daughter mentioned but not named—potential deus ex machina or future complication
Risk: chapter ends on question rather than revelation—reader satisfaction depends on next chapter quality
llm_fallback

state_changes.before: 自由身，决意离京归隐
state_changes.after: 被押入待罪院，等待定罪
state_changes.story_value: 从主动出走到被动囚禁，矛盾不可逆

state_changes.before: 试图以利诱达成政治联姻
state_changes.after: 勃然大怒，下令缉拿
state_changes.story_value: 和平手段彻底断绝，双方撕破脸

state_changes.before: 群情激愤，串联组织
state_changes.story_value: 个人事件升格为群体事件

state_changes.before: 主角逃离，危机暂缓
state_changes.after: 追兵赶上，危机再临
state_changes.story_value: 单点悬念接续群体悬念，双线并进

reader_rewards: 观看主角当众怒斥权相的正义凛然场面
见证落第举子挺身而出的义举
感受群体抗争的热血铺垫
获得主角命运悬念的持续张力
setups: 黄嵩的政治联姻提议
主角挂冠出逃的决定
冯公等主考的暗中支持态度
城外举子寓所的集会
午门三更的行动计划
payoffs: 主角拒绝弃妻的道德高地
主角被追兵捕获的危机
众举子三更午门集结
卢杞轿至午门遭伏击（待下回）
open_hooks: 主角能否脱罪或遭害
众举子殴打权相是否成功
冯公等主考的正式营救行动
梅良玉等进士的保本策略
reusable_mechanisms: 个人道义拒腐→触怒权贵→招致报复→群体动员→集体对抗的升级链
落难主角激起旁观者同理心→引发旁观者从被动嗟叹转为主动介入的转折
关键集会（酒席/密谈）作为行动前夜的叙事节点
门房/仆役作为信息传递和官方态度泄露的通道
'罪不及众'的宽慰作为隐性授权，鼓励参与者放手行动
权相仪仗（灯笼/轿子）作为公众辨识目标和行动信号
多事件同步推进：权相赴朝、举子集结、主考行动在同一时间节点交汇
群体行动前的最后确认和情绪调动

craft_observations.pov_distance: 全知视角广泛铺叙，多场景切换，主角视角与群体视角交替，保持情节推进的宏观视野
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: 对话节奏明快，宣言式独白（主角骂贼段落）占较长篇幅，情绪累积后突现行动描写
craft_observations.paragraphing: 长段对话与短促行动描写交替，用段落空白制造紧张节点
craft_observations.dialogue_method: 人物对话直接呈现，少量叙事补充；引用诗词作为道德定性标记
craft_observations.description_method: 场景转换依赖地名和时间词（'回到寓所''已交三鼓'），较少环境描写，重情节推进
craft_observations.exposition_method: 通过人物转述交代前情（黄嵩向卢杞汇报），保持叙事推进的流畅性
craft_observations.hook_delivery: 每段落尾设置小悬念（'只见后面旌旗遮日''众人递了个暗号'），整体章节以权相轿至午门作大悬念收尾
craft_observations.transition_method: '不说…再言…'的并置句式处理多线叙事，时间跳转依赖打更鼓声等标记
do_not_copy: 直接引用古典诗词作为道德标签的写法
'小畜生'等特定人物骂称
'吾把真心托明月'等特定人物引诗

non_reusable_specifics: 黄嵩的联姻三策
主角挂冠离京的具体行动
五城兵马五百名的追捕规模
城外举子寓所的十数坛酒、几十桌酒席
'太子太傅''卢府'灯笼标识
榜眼、进士等具体功名等级
risk_flags: 群体暴力行动（午门殴打权相）后续处置难度
主角命运完全悬于群体行动成功与否
多条叙事线（主角被囚/举子行动/主考策略）在单章内难以充分展开
政治敏感性：公然殴打宰相的场面处理
llm_fallback

chapter_function: Confrontation and initial judgment - corrupt officials exposed and ordered to trial, setting up deeper investigation into past crimes
scene_functions: Public beating scene at palace gate
Torture implements delivered via imperial edict

state_changes.before: Prime Minister controls the court and has imprisoned the scholar
state_changes.after: Prime Minister stripped of authority, ordered to face trial
state_changes.story_value: Justice begins to be served through imperial intervention

state_changes.before: Qiu Kui (榜眼) imprisoned in 待罪院 under guard
state_changes.after: Qiu Kui released and awaits justice at trial
state_changes.story_value: The innocent scholar gains hope of vindication

state_changes.before: Evil officials dominate, righteous officials silent
state_changes.after: Righteous officials now have imperial backing to prosecute
state_changes.story_value: Power balance shifts to the virtuous faction

state_changes.before: Emperor unaware of forced marriage scheme
state_changes.after: Emperor learns full truth, orders three-department investigation
state_changes.story_value: The conspiracy is brought to light

reader_rewards: Justice served as scholars publicly beat the corrupt officials at the palace gate
Full revelation of the forced marriage scheme exposing the Prime Minister's corruption
The Emperor's firm judgment ordering the corrupt officials to trial
Torture implements arrive signaling serious intent to extract confessions
Personal vendetta subplot introduced via the eunuch whose nephew was destroyed by the Prime Minister
Connection established between this case and previous victims (梅吏部, 陈东初)
setups: Qiu Kui's forced marriage scheme (setup in previous chapters)
Previous victims like 陈东初 who suffered under the Prime Minister (setup)
The scholar class's grievance against corrupt officials (setup)
payoffs: Scholars publicly confront and beat the corrupt officials at the gate
The forced marriage scheme is revealed to the Emperor
Three departments formally investigate with imperial authority
Torture implements delivered for interrogation
open_hooks: Will the three departments successfully extract confessions from the Prime Minister?
What happened to 梅吏部 and 陈东初 that connects to this case?
Will the Crown Prince's interference protect the corrupt officials?
What will the eunuch's personal vendetta reveal about the Prime Minister's other crimes?
How will the Prime Minister attempt to escape justice?
reusable_mechanisms: Mob justice delivered at palace gates by wronged parties
Emperor hearing arguments from both sides before rendering judgment
Inter-departmental joint trial system for major cases
Torture implements delivered via imperial decree signaling escalation
Eunuch character serving as witness to past crimes and agent of poetic justice
Confession extraction attempt revealing additional criminal cases

craft_observations.pov_distance: Third-person omniscient narrator with editorial distance, providing commentary on moral rightness of actions
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Alternates between rapid action sequences (beating scene) and measured dialogue (court scenes)
craft_observations.paragraphing: Long paragraphs for exposition and testimony, short punchy paragraphs for action beats
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Formal court language with emotional outbursts, officials speaking in official register
craft_observations.description_method: Focus on physical actions and status symbols rather than interiority
craft_observations.exposition_method: Complex backstory delivered through witness testimony rather than direct narration
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Cliffhanger ending with violence against the villain and uncertainty about consequences
craft_observations.transition_method: Scene transitions marked by character movements (arrival of officials, imperial summons)

non_reusable_specifics: 卢杞 (Prime Minister antagonist)
黄嵩 (Minister of Rites, co-conspirator)
邱魁/榜眼 (talented scholar forced to be son-in-law)
冯公 (righteous official, Chief Censor)
大理寺 (Court of Judicial Review)
刑部 (Ministry of Justice)
待罪院 (prison for awaiting judgment)
梅吏部 (previous victim mentioned)
陈东初 (previous victim, eunuch's connection)
黄太监 (eunuch with personal vendetta)
黄权 (eunuch's nephew who was destroyed)
黄上镇 (location of massacre)
榜眼 (second place in imperial examination)
三法司 (three judicial departments)
risk_flags: Political intrigue content - portrayal of corrupt officials
Violence against government officials
Trial and torture scenes
Potential for plot complications via Crown Prince's involvement

chapter_function: Climactic reward and reversal chapter transitioning from courtroom vindication to itinerant inspector journey, immediately deploying new authority against discovered corruption at first destination
scene_functions: Imperial edict scene (formal proclamation of rewards)
Funeral ceremony scene (public honor for father)
Discovery scene (secret shrine to disgraced minister revealed)

state_changes.before: Hidden avenger, unofficial position
state_changes.after: Empowered itinerant inspector with death authority
state_changes.story_value: Vindication and active power

state_changes.before: Executed traitor, defamed
state_changes.after: Rehabilitated with noble titles, temple, imperial memorial
state_changes.story_value: Family honor restored

state_changes.before: Believes himself secure in office
state_changes.after: Secret betrayal (temple worship of卢杞) exposed to protagonist
state_changes.story_value: Doom revealed

state_changes.before: Personal revenge dependent on circumstance
state_changes.after: Imperial mandate with execution authority over corruption
state_changes.story_value: Personal and official justice unified

reader_rewards: Formal edict scene satisfyingly enumerates all character rewards and promotions
Imperial recognition of all loyal characters' sacrifices
Protagonist granted supreme authority (上方宝剑,先斩后奏)
Immediate villain comeuppance upon arrival at destination
Protagonist begins journey with theatrical power and ceremony
setups: Protagonist's authority as itinerant inspector巡狩
Villain侯鸾's secret loyalty to disgraced卢杞
Protagonist's investigation method (disguised private inspection)
payoffs: Edict establishes all character fates definitively
Villain's secret temple immediately discovered upon arrival
Villain arrives to face judgment ignorant of exposure
open_hooks: Will protagonist execute or spare侯鸾?
What other corrupt officials will the circuit reveal?
How will protagonist's servant loyalty factor into proceedings?
The emperor's promised dual wedding at five phoenix pavilion
reusable_mechanisms: Imperial edict enumerating multiple character rewards (satisfies reader investment payoff)
Death-authority-granting scene establishing protagonist's power
Immediate deployment of authority upon arrival at new location
Discovery of villain's secret disloyalty through accidental finding
Funeral with full official attendance as public vindication ritual
Villain unaware of exposure while protagonist holds evidence

craft_observations.pov_distance: Third-person omniscient narrator maintains narrative distance through formal passages; switches to protagonist's direct perception during investigation scenes
craft_observations.sentence_rhythm: Edict uses formal parallel construction; dialogue alternates between protocol exchanges and urgent discovery moments; descriptive passages employ measured classical cadence
craft_observations.paragraphing: Scene breaks marked by chapter divisions; internal transitions use time markers (光阴迅速,不觉又) to compress off-screen periods
craft_observations.dialogue_method: Official scenes use protocol phrasing; discovery confrontation uses direct accusation-response; servant exchanges provide comic relief timing
craft_observations.description_method: Ceremonial scenes enumerate participants and objects catalog-fashion; investigation scenes use rapid sensory observation and interior monologue
craft_observations.exposition_method: Past events revealed through imperial edict reasoning and protagonist's speech recounting his history; backstory emerges through official justification
craft_observations.hook_delivery: Chapter ends mid-scene with villain kneeling in ignorance, creating immediate continuation impulse; each scene ends with forward momentum
craft_observations.transition_method: Time skips handled through formulaic phrases; location changes marked by movement verbs and arrival descriptions; emotional transitions occur through costume changes (孝服→朝服)
do_not_copy: Verse opening citations
Exact edict formal language patterns
Specific character name sequences

non_reusable_specifics: All character names (梅良玉,陈春生,卢杞,侯鸾, etc.)
Place names (真定府,大佛禅寺,五凤楼)
Specific titles and official ranks enumerated
The imperial edict's exact wording
Artifact details (上方宝剑,蟒衣,玉带)
The specific temple's layout and signage
Chapter title and verse epigraph
